cards.back: A technique used in distributed systems where a request is sent to a remote system to execute a procedure or function
A protocol used in remote invocation where a client sends a request to a server, and the server responds with the result of the request
A remote invocation technique where a program invokes a procedure or function on a remote system, as if it were running on the local system
A remote invocation technique where an object on a remote system invokes a method on another object, as if it were running on the same system
In RMI, there are two main components: the Client, which invokes the remote method, and the Server, which provides the remote method
